title: J-Invariant — identity through a full transformation cycle

condition.description: Close ANY full StateMachine cycle with lock dissolution: the same Cybernet that entered the cycle exits it (same name, same node — identity preserved through every mutation the steps performed), its ExecutionState turn_number advances to >= 2 (the only path that increments it is full-cycle closure), and no locked TraversalState remains on the walker (the lock dissolved rather than being abandoned mid-walk). This is the J-Invariance pattern made checkable: not same by form, not same by memory, same by J.

body.query: MATCH (c:Cybernet {name: $walker})-[:HAS_LIFECYCLE]->(es:ExecutionState) WHERE es.turn_number >= 2 AND NOT (c)-[:HAS_TRAVERSAL]->(:TraversalState {status: 'locked'}) RETURN c.name AS cybernet, es.equipped_sm_id AS state_machine, es.turn_number AS turn_number

check.earned_when: the response is a non-empty array — the walker has at least one ExecutionState with turn_number >= 2 and carries no locked TraversalState.
check.verified_against_live_api: 2026-06-12 — the unparameterized form returned JaniScribe (janic_cycle_sm, turn_number 4) and Jani_Prime (janic_cycle_sm, turn_number 2) live

earned_by.evidence: live ExecutionState: equipped_sm_id janic_cycle_sm, turn_number 2, no locked TraversalState — read 2026-06-12 via POST /api/query.
live ExecutionState: equipped_sm_id janic_cycle_sm, turn_number 4, no locked TraversalState — read 2026-06-12 via POST /api/query.
